refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and adaptive control. This supports both Site Manager acceptance criteria and Operator/Tele-operator decision-making.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_mass_moved_tons) over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 1 week of representative operations). Compare against E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and task logs from Tasking interface. Validate across multiple operators and site conditions.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tons moved) at sub-minute granularity.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator/tele-operator control strategies that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager for acceptance validation and continuous improvement.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy monitoring and KPI calculation in Vehicle power management subsystem; expose energy_per_ton metric via Telemetry interface; provide operator feedback through Vehicle HMI and Teleoperation interface.

DP-B: Centralize energy efficiency monitoring in a dedicated Energy Management Service (external to Vehicle); subscribe to Vehicle telemetry; compute and broadcast KPI via Telemetry; integrate with Tasking for work-output correlation.

DP-C: Distribute energy monitoring across Vehicle (raw power measurement), Teleoperation (KPI aggregation), and Safety Services (threshold alerting); coordinate via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces.

DP-D: Implement energy efficiency as a closed-loop control system: Vehicle measures power and work; Teleoperation receives telemetry, computes KPI, sends control recommendations back to Vehicle via Tasking; operator applies recommendations.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement energy efficiency as a closed-loop control system where the Vehicle measures power consumption and work output (tons moved), transmits raw telemetry to Teleoperation; Teleoperation computes energy_per_ton KPI in real-time, provides feedback to operator/tele-operator, and sends optimized control recommendations back to Vehicle via Tasking interface. Safety Services monitors energy efficiency thresholds and issues SafetyAlert if efficiency degrades below acceptable bounds (indicating potential fault or unsafe operation).

potential_sources: Vehicle power measurement accuracy couples to Teleoperation KPI calculation; mitigation: define power measurement calibration standard.
Work-output quantification (tons moved) couples to Tasking interface definition; mitigation: standardize work-unit reporting in Tasking protocol.
Feedback loop latency couples to operator response time and safety; mitigation: define maximum acceptable latency (e.g., <2 sec) and monitor via SafetyAlert.
Energy efficiency target (E_target) is customer-defined; couples to acceptance criteria; mitigation: require explicit E_target specification in contract before decomposition.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D introduces a feedback loop (Vehicle → Teleoperation → Vehicle via Tasking), which is more complex than DP-A or DP-B but enables adaptive control and real-time optimization. Coupling is manageable if interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are well-specified. DP-D is preferred over DP-C (distributed) because it centralizes KPI logic in Teleoperation, reducing diffusion and improving consistency.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle power measurement subsystem can reliably measure instantaneous power consumption (kW) with ±5% accuracy.
ASSUMPTION: Tasking interface supports bi-directional communication (Vehicle → Teleoperation for telemetry; Teleoperation → Vehicle for control recommendations).
ASSUMPTION: Operator/tele-operator can interpret and act on energy efficiency feedback within acceptable response time (<2 sec).
RISK: If Vehicle power measurement is inaccurate or drifts, KPI will be unreliable, undermining acceptance criteria. Mitigation: implement periodic calibration and drift detection.
RISK: If Teleoperation KPI computation is delayed or lossy, operator feedback will be stale, reducing effectiveness. Mitigation: define latency SLA and implement buffering/smoothing.
RISK: Operator may not have sufficient training or incentive to optimize for energy efficiency. Mitigation: integrate energy KPI into operator performance metrics and provide training.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify in DP-D the definition of 'work output (tons moved)': is it based on load cell measurement, GPS displacement + estimated mass, or task-level reporting via Tasking? This affects measurement accuracy and coupling to Tasking interface. Recommend: define work-output measurement method as part of DP refinement and validate against E_target acceptance criteria.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the energy efficiency KPI computation, real-time feedback generation, and control recommendation logic. It receives raw telemetry (power, work output) from Vehicle via Telemetry interface, computes energy_per_ton, displays KPI to tele-operator, and sends optimized control recommendations back to Vehicle via Tasking interface. It also interfaces with Safety Services via SafetyAlert for threshold monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives Vehicle power consumption (kW), work output (tons), and operational state from Vehicle. Used to compute energy_per_ton KPI in real-time.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends control recommendations (e.g., speed adjustments, load optimization) to Vehicle to improve energy efficiency. Bi-directional: also receives task status and work-output updates.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives energy efficiency threshold alerts from Safety Services (e.g., if energy_per_ton exceeds E_target + margin). May send efficiency-related alerts to Safety Services if anomalies detected.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying communication channel for all telemetry, tasking, and safety alert exchanges between Teleoperation and Vehicle/Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. Energy efficiency KPI logic could reside here, but it would be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Not recommended as primary owner; better as orchestrator.

Vehicle owns power measurement and work-output sensing. However, KPI computation (energy_per_ton) and operator feedback logic are better suited to Teleoperation, which has direct operator interface and can aggregate data from multiple vehicles. Assigning KPI logic to Vehicle would duplicate it across vehicle instances.

Teleoperation is the natural owner of KPI computation, real-time feedback, and control recommendations. It has direct interface to tele-operator, can aggregate telemetry from Vehicle, and can send optimized tasking back. Minimal diffusion: all energy efficiency logic is centralized here. Recommended primary owner.

Safety Services could own energy efficiency threshold monitoring and alerting (e.g., if energy_per_ton exceeds safe bounds, indicating potential fault). However, primary KPI computation and operator feedback should reside in Teleoperation. Safety Services acts as a secondary monitor/validator.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation is the optimal owner of energy efficiency KPI logic because: (1) it has direct interface to tele-operator for feedback; (2) it can aggregate telemetry from Vehicle without duplication; (3) it can send control recommendations via Tasking; (4) it centralizes KPI computation, reducing diffusion and improving consistency. Vehicle owns raw measurement (power, work output); Safety Services owns threshold monitoring and alerting.
containment_risks: RISK: If Teleoperation module is overloaded with KPI computation, it may impact latency of other teleoperation functions (e.g., real-time vehicle control). Mitigation: implement KPI computation as a separate thread/process with bounded latency SLA.

RISK: If Telemetry interface does not provide sufficient granularity (e.g., power averaged over 1 sec instead of instantaneous), KPI accuracy will suffer. Mitigation: define Telemetry interface specification to include power sampling rate and work-output update frequency.

RISK: If Tasking interface does not support bi-directional communication or has high latency, control recommendations will be ineffective. Mitigation: validate Tasking interface design for latency and bidirectionality.

RISK: If Vehicle power measurement drifts or becomes inaccurate, Teleoperation KPI will be unreliable. Mitigation: implement power measurement validation and calibration checks in Vehicle; alert Teleoperation if measurement quality degrades.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the Teleoperation module boundary to explicitly separate: (1) Energy Efficiency KPI Computation (owns energy_per_ton calculation, logging, and reporting); (2) Operator Feedback & Control Recommendation (owns HMI display and tasking generation). This sub-modularization improves testability and allows independent evolution. Also, clarify the interface contract for Telemetry (sampling rate, accuracy, latency) and Tasking (control recommendation format, response time) to ensure KPI computation is feasible.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (closed-loop control system) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling continuous measurement, real-time feedback, and adaptive control to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target. The DP specifies the logical solution (feedback loop, KPI computation, control recommendations) without over-constraining implementation. Coupling is manageable with well-defined interfaces.

diffusion_risks: Energy efficiency KPI logic is centralized in Teleoperation (low diffusion), but Vehicle power measurement and work-output sensing are distributed in Vehicle. This is acceptable because measurement is a distinct concern from KPI computation.
Safety Services monitors energy efficiency thresholds (SafetyAlert), which is a secondary concern and does not duplicate Teleoperation's primary KPI logic.
No duplication of KPI computation across modules. Teleoperation is the single source of truth for energy_per_ton metric.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module containment score is 0.85, indicating strong fit. The module owns the primary KPI logic and has direct operator interface. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all known and available. No new modules or interfaces need to be invented. Ownership is clear: Teleoperation is responsible for energy efficiency KPI delivery.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-003 is feasible via measurement of energy_per_ton over an evaluation period. Teleoperation module can log energy consumption (from Telemetry) and work output (from Tasking), compute KPI, and report to Site Manager. Acceptance criteria (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) can be validated against logged data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Teleoperation owns KPI computation and feedback; Vehicle owns power/work measurement; Safety Services owns threshold alerting. Each module has a distinct responsibility. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). Traceability from FR to DP to IM is established.

recommended_revisions: Refine Telemetry interface specification to define power sampling rate (recommend ≥1 Hz for accurate KPI), work-output update frequency, and measurement accuracy (recommend ±5% for power, ±10% for work output).
Refine Tasking interface specification to define control recommendation format, latency SLA (recommend <2 sec), and bidirectionality requirements.
Define E_target value explicitly in contract before proceeding to detailed design. E_target is a customer-defined acceptance criterion and must be known to validate KPI computation logic.
Specify power measurement calibration procedure in Vehicle module to ensure long-term accuracy and detect drift.
Define operator training requirements for interpreting and acting on energy efficiency feedback.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (measurement, feedback, control, reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define detailed DPs and IMs. The selected DP-D (closed-loop control system) is logically sound and the assigned IM (Teleoperation) is well-justified, but the next level of decomposition is necessary to address sub-FRs and define detailed module responsibilities, interface contracts, and verification criteria.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tons moved) at sub-minute granularity.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator/tele-operator control strategies that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency metrics to Site Manager for acceptance validation and continuous improvement.

assumptions: Vehicle power measurement subsystem can reliably measure instantaneous power consumption (kW) with ±5% accuracy.
Tasking interface supports bi-directional communication (Vehicle ↔ Teleoperation).
Operator/tele-operator can interpret and act on energy efficiency feedback within <2 sec response time.
E_target (customer-defined energy efficiency threshold) is specified in contract before detailed design.
Work output (tons moved) can be measured or inferred from Vehicle sensors and task data with ±10% accuracy.
Safety criticality of energy efficiency is high; threshold violations must trigger SafetyAlert within <1 sec.
open_risks: Vehicle power measurement accuracy and drift over time; mitigation: implement calibration and validation checks.
Telemetry interface latency and granularity; mitigation: define interface SLA (sampling rate ≥1 Hz, latency <500 ms).
Operator training and incentive to optimize for energy efficiency; mitigation: integrate KPI into performance metrics and provide training.
Feedback loop stability and control recommendation effectiveness; mitigation: validate control algorithms via simulation and field testing.
Integration of energy efficiency KPI with safety constraints (e.g., cannot reduce speed below safe minimum); mitigation: define safety-energy trade-off rules in control logic.
Scalability of KPI computation if multiple vehicles are monitored; mitigation: design Teleoperation module for parallel processing.
evidence_needed: Vehicle power measurement specification and calibration procedure.
Telemetry interface detailed specification (sampling rate, accuracy, latency, data format).
Tasking interface detailed specification (control recommendation format, latency, bidirectionality).
SafetyAlert interface specification for energy efficiency threshold monitoring.
Customer-defined E_target value and evaluation period.
Operator training plan and performance metrics integration.
Control algorithm design and validation (simulation, field testing).
Energy efficiency KPI computation logic (formula, edge cases, error handling).
Acceptance test plan for CR-NGHE-003 (measurement methodology, success criteria).
